mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding economic activity, which increases demand for capital and pushes long-term yields upward. The effect is monotonic and persistent across most regimes.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between monthly changes in INDPRO and subsequent 20-day yield changes is not consistently positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is above 6%, labor market slack indicates weak aggregate demand. Investors seek safe-haven assets, pushing down the 10-year Treasury yield as a flight-to-quality trade intensifies.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6%, the average 20-day yield change is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real federal funds rate, where FEDFUNDS is below trailing CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL), erodes the real return of bonds. Investors demand higher nominal yields to compensate for inflation risk, pushing yields up over the next month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL 12-month change is negative for at least two consecutive months, subsequent 20-day yield changes are not positive in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions, robust GDP growth (GDPC1) leads to higher real yields as the Federal Reserve eventually tightens policy. The signal is regime-conditional: positive GDP growth is necessary for the yield-increasing effect.
failure_condition: In quarters with positive GDP growth, the 20-day yield change is not positive more often than not over the following quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.2 million units indicate robust demand in the housing sector, which feeds into broader economic strength and pushes long-term yields higher as investors anticipate stronger growth and inflation.
failure_condition: When monthly housing starts exceed 1200 thousand units, the subsequent 20-day yield change is not positive in a majority of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains (PAYEMS) combined with a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal intensifying labor market tightness. This raises inflation expectations and bond yields as the market prices in a higher inflation premium.
failure_condition: When monthly PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases in the same month, the 20-day yield change is not positive in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Short-term increases in industrial production reflect immediate economic momentum. Investors quickly adjust near-term growth expectations, leading to a rise in yields within a week.
failure_condition: The 5-day yield change following a monthly INDPRO increase is not positive in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2%, the Federal Reserve is in an active tightening cycle. Higher short-term rates propagate through the term structure, lifting the 10-year yield over a quarter horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 2%, the 60-day yield change is not positive in a majority of observations over any 5-year window.
